heading: Reportable food registry
label: 350f Reportable food registry
notes: References in TextSection 350d(a)(3), (4) of this title, referred to in subsecs. (e)(1) and (k), was redesignated section 350d(a)(4), (5), respectively, of this title by , , .Pub. L. 111–353, title I, § 102(a)(2)124 Stat. 3887Jan. 4, 2011

Construction of 2011 AmendmentNothing in amendment by to be construed to apply to certain alcohol-related facilities, to alter jurisdiction and authorities established under certain other Acts, or in a manner inconsistent with international agreements to which the United States is a party, see sections 2206, 2251, and 2252 of this title.Pub. Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–85, title X, § 1005(a)121 Stat. 964Sept. 27, 2007“Congress makes the following findings:“(1)In 1994, Congress passed the Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act of 1994 () [see Short Title of 1994 Amendments note set out under ] to provide the Food and Drug Administration the legal framework which is intended to ensure that dietary supplements are safe and properly labeled foods.Public Law 103–417section 301 of this title“(2)In 2006, Congress passed the Dietary Supplement and Nonprescription Drug Consumer Protection Act () [see Short Title of 2006 Amendment note set out under ] to establish a mandatory reporting system of serious adverse events for nonprescription drugs and dietary supplements sold and consumed in the United States.Public Law 109–462section 301 of this title“(3)The adverse event reporting system created under the Dietary Supplement and Nonprescription Drug Consumer Protection Act is intended to serve as an early warning system for potential public health issues associated with the use of these products.“(4)A reliable mechanism to track patterns of adulteration in food would support efforts by the Food and Drug Administration to target limited inspection resources to protect the public health.”

Guidance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–85, title X, § 1005(f)121 Stat. 969Sept. 27, 2007“Not later than 9 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of Health and Human Services] shall issue a guidance to industry about submitting reports to the electronic portal established under section 417 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] (as added by this section) and providing notifications to other persons in the supply chain of an article of food under such section 417.”Sept. 27, 200721 U.S.C. 350f

children.heading: Responsible party
children.label: 1 Responsible party
children.text: The term “responsible party”, with respect to an article of food, means a person that submits the registration under for a food facility that is required to register under , at which such article of food is manufactured, processed, packed, or held.section 350d(a) of this titlesection 350d(a) of this title

children.heading: Reportable food
children.label: 2 Reportable food
children.text: The term “reportable food” means an article of food (other than infant formula) for which there is a reasonable probability that the use of, or exposure to, such article of food will cause serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals.

children.label: A
children.text: Federal, State, and local public health officials; or

children.label: B
children.text: responsible parties.

children.heading: Review by Secretary
children.label: 2 Review by Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall promptly review and assess the information submitted under paragraph (1) for the purposes of identifying reportable food, submitting entries to the Reportable Food Registry, acting under subsection (c), and exercising other existing food safety authorities under this chapter to protect the public health.

children.heading: Issuance of an alert by the Secretary
children.label: c Issuance of an alert by the Secretary

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall issue, or cause to be issued, an alert or a notification with respect to a reportable food using information from the Reportable Food Registry as the Secretary deems necessary to protect the public health.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 2 Effect
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not affect the authority of the Secretary to issue an alert or a notification under any other provision of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: submit a report to the Food and Drug Administration through the electronic portal established under subsection (b) that includes the data elements described in subsection (e) (except the elements described in paragraphs (8), (9), and (10) of such subsection); and

children.label: B
children.text: investigate the cause of the adulteration if the adulteration of the article of food may have originated with the responsible party.

children.label: A
children.text: the adulteration originated with the responsible party;

children.label: B
children.text: the responsible party detected the adulteration prior to any transfer to another person of such article of food; and

children.label: i
children.text: corrected such adulteration; or

children.label: ii
children.text: destroyed or caused the destruction of such article of food.

children.heading: Reports by public health officials
children.label: 3 Reports by public health officials
children.text: A Federal, State, or local public health official may submit a report about a reportable food to the Food and Drug Administration through the electronic portal established under subsection (b) that includes the data elements described in subsection (e) that the official is able to provide.

children.heading: Report number
children.label: 4 Report number
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that, upon submission of a report under paragraph (1) or (3), a unique number is issued through the electronic portal established under subsection (b) to the person submitting such report, by which the Secretary is able to link reports about the reportable food submitted and amended under this subsection and identify the supply chain for such reportable food.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 5 Review
children.text: The Secretary shall promptly review a report submitted under paragraph (1) or (3).

children.heading: Response to report submitted by a responsible party
children.label: 6 Response to report submitted by a responsible party
A
children.text: Amend the report submitted by the responsible party under paragraph (1) to include the data element described in subsection (e)(9).

children.label: i
children.text: to the immediate previous source of the article of food, if the Secretary deems necessary;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the immediate subsequent recipient of the article of food, if the Secretary deems necessary; and

children.label: I
children.text: the data elements described in subsection (e) that the Secretary deems necessary;

children.label: II
children.text: the actions described under paragraph (7) that the recipient of the notification shall perform, as required by the Secretary; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other information that the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: Submit a report to the Food and Drug Administration through the electronic portal established under subsection (b) that includes those data elements described in subsection (e) and other information that the Secretary deems necessary.

children.label: B
children.text: Investigate the cause of the adulteration if the adulteration of the article of food may have originated with the responsible party.

children.label: i
children.text: to the immediate previous source of the article of food, if the Secretary deems necessary;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the immediate subsequent recipient of the article of food, if the Secretary deems necessary; and

children.label: I
children.text: the data elements described in subsection (e) that the Secretary deems necessary;

children.label: II
children.text: the actions described under this paragraph that the recipient of the notification shall perform, as required by the Secretary; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other information that the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the responsible party is not required to submit an additional report or make a notification under paragraph (7); and

children.label: B
children.text: the responsible party shall amend the report submitted by the responsible party under paragraph (1) to include the data elements described in paragraph (9), and, with respect to both such notification and such report, paragraph (11) of subsection (e).
The registration numbers of the responsible party under section 350d(a)(3) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1
The date on which an article of food was determined to be a reportable food.
A description of the article of food including the quantity or amount.
The extent and nature of the adulteration.

If the adulteration of the article of food may have originated with the responsible party, the results of the investigation required under paragraph (1)(B) or (7)(B) of subsection (d), as applicable and when known.
The disposition of the article of food, when known.
Product information typically found on packaging including product codes, use-by dates, and names of manufacturers, packers, or distributors sufficient to identify the article of food.
Contact information for the responsible party.
The contact information for parties directly linked in the supply chain and notified under paragraph (6)(B) or (7)(C) of subsection (d), as applicable.

The information required by the Secretary to be included in a notification provided by the responsible party involved under paragraph (6)(B) or (7)(C) of subsection (d) or required in a report under subsection (d)(7)(A).
The unique number described in subsection (d)(4).
a description of the article of food as provided in subsection (e)(3);
as provided in subsection (e)(7), affected product identification codes, such as UPC, SKU, or lot or batch numbers sufficient for the consumer to identify the article of food;
contact information for the responsible party as provided in subsection (e)(8); and
any other information the Secretary determines is necessary to enable a consumer to accurately identify whether such consumer is in possession of the reportable food.

children.label: A
children.text: prepare the critical information described under subsection (f) for a reportable food as a standardized one-page summary;

children.label: B
children.text: publish such one-page summary on the Internet website of the Food and Drug Administration in a format that can be easily printed by a grocery store for purposes of consumer notification.

children.heading: Action by grocery store
children.label: 2 Action by grocery store
children.text: A notification described under paragraph (1)(B) shall include the date and time such summary was posted on the Internet website of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a grocery store sold a reportable food that is the subject of the posting and such establishment is part of chain of establishments with 15 or more physical locations, then such establishment shall, not later than 24 hours after a one page summary described in subsection (g) is published, prominently display such summary or the information from such summary via at least one of the methods identified under paragraph (2) and maintain the display for 14 days.2So in original. Probably should be followed by “a”.2

children.label: A
children.text: posting the notification at or near the register;

children.label: B
children.text: providing the location of the reportable food;

children.label: C
children.text: providing targeted recall information given to customers upon purchase of a food; and

children.label: D
children.text: other such prominent and conspicuous locations and manners utilized by grocery stores as of , to provide notice of such recalls to consumers as considered appropriate by the Secretary.January 4, 2011

children.heading: Coordination of Federal, State, and local efforts
children.label: i Coordination of Federal, State, and local efforts
A
children.text: share information and coordinate regulatory efforts with the Department of Agriculture; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the Secretary receives a report submitted about a food within the jurisdiction of the Department of Agriculture, promptly provide such report to the Department of Agriculture.

children.label: A
children.text: help to ensure coverage of the safety of the food supply chain, including those food establishments regulated by the States and localities that are not required to register under ; andsection 350d of this title

children.label: B
children.text: reduce duplicative regulatory efforts.

children.heading: Maintenance and inspection of records
children.label: j Maintenance and inspection of records
children.text: The responsible party shall maintain records related to each report received, notification made, and report submitted to the Food and Drug Administration under this section for 2 years. A responsible party shall, at the request of the Secretary, permit inspection of such records as provided for section 350c of this title.3So in original. Probably should be “in section”.3

children.heading: Request for information
children.label: k Request for information
children.text: Except as provided by section 350d(a)(4) of this title, shall apply to any request for information regarding a record in the Reportable Food Registry.1section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Safety report
children.label: l Safety report
children.text: A report or notification under subsection (d) shall be considered to be a safety report under and may be accompanied by a statement, which shall be part of any report released for public disclosure, that denies that the report or the notification constitutes an admission that the product involved caused or contributed to a death, serious injury, or serious illness.section 379v of this title

children.heading: Admission
children.label: m Admission
children.text: A report or notification under this section shall not be considered an admission that the article of food involved is adulterated or caused or contributed to a death, serious injury, or serious illness.

children.heading: Homeland Security notification
children.label: n Homeland Security notification
children.text: If, after receiving a report under subsection (d), the Secretary believes such food may have been deliberately adulterated, the Secretary shall immediately notify the Secretary of Homeland Security. The Secretary shall make relevant information from the Reportable Food Registry available to the Secretary of Homeland Security.
